title: Jalandhar to get very hot soon, temperature likely to touch 42°C; KNOW when - True Scoop
full_text:
Weather in Jalandhar is set for a dramatic shift, as data recorded on April 8 indicates a temporary rainy phase followed by a sharp and continuous rise in temperature that may push the mercury up to 42°C in the coming days.
As per the latest forecast data observed on April 8, Jalandhar is currently witnessing light to moderate rainfall, with temperatures around 15°C and a precipitation probability of nearly 75%. This has brought short-term relief to residents, making conditions cooler and more comfortable compared to recent days.
However, the same April 8 forecast data clearly outlines a rapid transition beginning from April 9 onwards. The weather is expected to turn completely clear, with bright sunshine dominating the skies. Temperatures will begin climbing steadily, with maximum levels reaching around 26°C on April 9 and further increasing to 29°C by April 10 and April 11.
By April 13 and April 14, daytime temperatures are expected to hover around 30°C, signaling the gradual buildup of heat across the region. The situation becomes more intense midweek, when the mercury is predicted to cross 34°C on April 15 and rise to approximately 35°C on April 16.
The most significant spike is expected towards the end of the forecast cycle. By April 17, temperatures may surge dramatically, reaching as high as 42°C. This sharp jump indicates the likely onset of strong heatwave conditions across Jalandhar and nearby areas.
Meanwhile, air quality data noted on April 8 shows an AQI level of 91, which falls under the “satisfactory” category. While pollution is currently under control, rising temperatures and dry winds in the coming days could influence air quality levels.
Residents are advised to prepare for extreme heat conditions ahead. Staying hydrated, avoiding outdoor exposure during peak afternoon hours, and taking precautions for vulnerable groups such as children and elderly individuals will be essential.
In conclusion, April 8 weather data clearly highlights a sharp transition phase for Jalandhar—from rain-cooled conditions to an approaching intense heatwave, with temperatures potentially peaking at 42°C in the days ahead.
